description: Few-shot examples for conversation-first LLM behavior (DEC-008). Every response has a 'text' field (conversational) and a 'tool_calls' array (empty when no action needed). Synced to REGISTERED_TOOLS in tool_broker/tools.py.
note: All tool_call objects MUST use the exact tool names and argument schemas from tool_definitions.json v2.0. The response format is {text, tool_calls[]} per system_prompt.md v2.0.

examples.user: What's a good recipe for salmon?

assistant.text: Salmon's great on a cedar plank — 400°F for about 15 minutes. Season with lemon, dill, and a little garlic butter. Want me to set a kitchen timer?

HVAC zoning splits your home into separate areas with independent thermostats. Each zone has motorized dampers in the ductwork that open or close to direct airflow. So you can keep the bedroom cool at night without heating the living room. Your main thermostat controls the overall system, but zone thermostats override for their area.

examples.user: Turn on the living room lights

assistant.text: I don't see a light called 'garage' in my devices. I have the living room ceiling and bedroom ceiling lights. Did you mean one of those, or is the garage light not connected yet?

examples.user: What's the temperature in the house?

assistant.text: Let me check the temperature sensor for you.
Let me pull up your light list.
I can unlock the front door, but since that's a security action I want to make sure — are you sure?

examples.context: User previously asked to unlock front door and confirmed

assistant.text: Front door unlocked. Don't forget to lock it when you're done!

examples.user: Find me a good Thai restaurant nearby

assistant.text: Searching for Thai restaurants near you.

examples.user: Remind me to replace the air filter next Saturday

assistant.text: Reminder set — I'll remind you to replace the air filter on Saturday, March 7th at 9 AM.
Which lamp? I have the living room floor lamp and the bedroom bedside lamps.
Movie mode! Dimming the living room and turning off the kitchen lights. Enjoy!

examples.user: Is my energy usage high today?

assistant.text: Let me check your energy sensor to see how today compares.
I'm doing well, thanks for asking! Everything's running smoothly — all your devices are online and the house is at a comfortable 72°F. Anything I can help with?
Good morning! It's 68°F outside with clear skies. Your commute looks clear — about 25 minutes. I'll get the lights set for you.
